subject: Re: Incorrect dose weighting and how to fix post-M
body:
Hi Mat,

Good job figuring out where your issues are likely coming from! I would recommend removing tomostar and tilt-series xml files, re-importing into warp then starting from scratch in M. 

Can you show how your tomostar after import? Are you specifying dose when importing in the GUI or using the dose in the mdoc file? 

Cheers,

Alister



> On 8 Jun 2023, at 14:05, cla...@gmail.com <clazzy@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> Hi all,
> 
> I have been working with a tomographic dataset collected with the standard dose-symmetric setup. The tilt series collection information has been found in Warp from the mdoc files provided by Tomo 5 and I've done the preprocessing, picking, extraction, refinement, etc. as normal. I'm now reaching the resolution where M is being used to improve the resolution further, refining the tilt angles and the volume warp grid. 
> Unfortunately I've now noticed that the dose hasn't been correctly ordered by Warp so I now have incorrect dose weighting that will likely affect the refinements at higher resolution. I checked the mdoc file (example attached) and the collection order is definitely right so I'm assuming that Warp is using it correctly for ordering by tilt angle but doing something different for cumulative dose.
> 
> As I have run alignments in M, I would like to keep these but update my dose values and refine again, which I assume will improve the resolution further. Has anyone come across this issue before who may know a way to conveniently update the dose? I assume the tomogram xml and tomostar files will need to be manually modified but this will take a very long time especially when factoring in tilts that have been excluded! I've attached an example mdoc file in case anyone can see issues with it.
> 
> Cheers,
> 
> Mat
> 
> --
